author: runner-7
rationale: Input pin only (P2 P-9: a PINNED-AT-INPUTS placeholder completed by ops amendment after the judge-1p run and before the stage-1 final-phase record; no design field is touched). The judge-1p STAND-IN run (GPT-5.6-Sol via npx-pinned @openai/codex 0.144.1, reasoning effort low) completed 360/360 real d-qa-t items in the judge-1 role shuffle dadjt/1|judge-1|20260710 then 60/60 deranged-probe items in dadjt/1|judge-1p-probe|20260711 (0 no-label, 0 transport retries; preflight PASS), and data/d-adj-t-llmproxy/ was assembled: judge-1p-responses.jsonl, judge-1p-probe-responses.jsonl, labels-proxy.jsonl (gold source judge-1p ALONE; membership gold = d-qa-t answer; escape = disagreement), and summary.json (the exact analysis-input integers per analysis/f2b_transfer_llmproxy.py). This ops amendment resolves the 'PINNED-AT-INPUTS:judge-1p-responses+probe-responses+labels-proxy+summary-pinned-by-ops-amendment-after-the-judge-1p-run-and-before-the-stage-1-record' placeholder in pins.corpus_hashes to the kot-corpus-hash/1 digest of data/d-adj-t-llmproxy/ (tools/registry/corpus-pin.py d-adj-t-llmproxy), covering all 9 corpus files including the run outputs. RT-14 account_lint over the corpus is clean (the only @ is the npm package spec @openai/codex@0.144.1). One-shot pin discipline: any change to those bytes before the final-phase run invalidates this value and requires a pre-run correction record, not a new ops amendment.
